case_notes: Two-way: Dativ = location/rest, Akkusativ = movement toward

example_sentences.de: Ich stehe an der Tür.
example_sentences.en: I am standing at the door.

example_sentences.de: Er geht an die Tafel.
example_sentences.en: He goes to the board.

case_notes: Two-way: Dativ = location on a surface, Akkusativ = movement onto a surface

example_sentences.de: Das Buch liegt auf dem Tisch.
example_sentences.en: The book is on the table.

example_sentences.de: Ich lege das Buch auf den Tisch.
example_sentences.en: I put the book on the table.

case_notes: Two-way: Dativ = location inside, Akkusativ = movement into

example_sentences.de: Sie wohnt in der Stadt.
example_sentences.en: She lives in the city.

example_sentences.de: Wir fahren in die Stadt.
example_sentences.en: We are driving into the city.

case_notes: Always Dativ. Used for accompaniment and means of transport.

example_sentences.de: Ich fahre mit dem Bus.
example_sentences.en: I travel by bus.

example_sentences.de: Sie kommt mit ihrer Freundin.
example_sentences.en: She comes with her friend.

case_notes: Always Dativ. Used for origin, possession (instead of Genitiv in spoken German), and authorship.

example_sentences.de: Das ist ein Buch von Goethe.
example_sentences.en: That is a book by Goethe.

example_sentences.de: Ich komme von der Arbeit.
example_sentences.en: I'm coming from work.

case_notes: Always Dativ. Used for movement to people/places (not countries), purpose, and time expressions.

example_sentences.de: Ich gehe zu meinem Freund.
example_sentences.en: I'm going to my friend's place.

example_sentences.de: Wir essen zu Mittag.
example_sentences.en: We eat at noon.

case_notes: Always Dativ. Used for origin (cities/countries) and movement out of enclosed spaces.

example_sentences.de: Er kommt aus Deutschland.
example_sentences.en: He comes from Germany.

example_sentences.de: Sie geht aus dem Zimmer.
example_sentences.en: She goes out of the room.

case_notes: Always Dativ. Used for location near a place/person, or for workplace.

example_sentences.de: Ich bin beim Arzt.
example_sentences.en: I am at the doctor's.

example_sentences.de: Er wohnt bei seinen Eltern.
example_sentences.en: He lives with his parents.

case_notes: Always Dativ. Used for movement to cities/countries/home, time (after), and expressing order/opinion.

example_sentences.de: Wir fahren nach Berlin.
example_sentences.en: We are going to Berlin.

example_sentences.de: Nach dem Essen gehen wir spazieren.
example_sentences.en: After the meal we go for a walk.

case_notes: Always Dativ. Used with ongoing actions that started in the past and continue to the present. Used with present tense in German (not past).

example_sentences.de: Ich wohne seit drei Jahren hier.
example_sentences.en: I have been living here for three years.

example_sentences.de: Sie lernt seit dem Sommer Deutsch.
example_sentences.en: She has been learning German since summer.

case_notes: Always Akkusativ. Used for purpose, benefit, duration, and exchange.

example_sentences.de: Das Geschenk ist für dich.
example_sentences.en: The gift is for you.

example_sentences.de: Ich lerne für die Prüfung.
example_sentences.en: I am studying for the exam.

case_notes: Always Akkusativ. Used for movement through a space or means by which something is achieved.

example_sentences.de: Wir gehen durch den Park.
example_sentences.en: We walk through the park.

example_sentences.de: Er lernte Deutsch durch Filme.
example_sentences.en: He learned German through films.

case_notes: Always Akkusativ. Expresses absence or exclusion.

example_sentences.de: Ich trinke Kaffee ohne Zucker.
example_sentences.en: I drink coffee without sugar.

example_sentences.de: Sie fährt ohne ihren Mann.
example_sentences.en: She travels without her husband.

case_notes: Always Akkusativ. Used for movement around something, clock times, and approximation.

example_sentences.de: Der Zug fährt um acht Uhr ab.
example_sentences.en: The train departs at eight o'clock.

example_sentences.de: Wir gehen um den See.
example_sentences.en: We walk around the lake.

case_notes: Always Akkusativ. Used for time limits and spatial endpoints. Often combined with other prepositions (bis zu, bis in).

example_sentences.de: Der Laden ist bis achtzehn Uhr geöffnet.
example_sentences.en: The shop is open until 6 p.m.

example_sentences.de: Ich bleibe bis nächste Woche.
example_sentences.en: I am staying until next week.

case_notes: Two-way: Dativ = location above/over, Akkusativ = movement over or concerning (topic).

example_sentences.de: Das Bild hängt über dem Bett.
example_sentences.en: The picture hangs above the bed.

example_sentences.de: Wir sprechen über das Problem.
example_sentences.en: We are talking about the problem.

case_notes: Two-way: Dativ = location below/among, Akkusativ = movement to a position below.

example_sentences.de: Die Katze schläft unter dem Tisch.
example_sentences.en: The cat sleeps under the table.

example_sentences.de: Er stellt die Kiste unter den Tisch.
example_sentences.en: He puts the box under the table.

case_notes: Two-way: Dativ = location in front of / time expressions (vor drei Tagen), Akkusativ = movement to position in front of.

example_sentences.de: Das Auto steht vor dem Haus.
example_sentences.en: The car is parked in front of the house.

example_sentences.de: Sie stellt die Vase vor das Fenster.
example_sentences.en: She puts the vase in front of the window.

case_notes: Two-way: Dativ = location behind, Akkusativ = movement to a position behind.

example_sentences.de: Der Garten ist hinter dem Haus.
example_sentences.en: The garden is behind the house.

example_sentences.de: Er stellt das Fahrrad hinter das Haus.
example_sentences.en: He puts the bicycle behind the house.

case_notes: Two-way: Dativ = location beside, Akkusativ = movement to a position beside.

example_sentences.de: Er sitzt neben mir.
example_sentences.en: He sits next to me.

example_sentences.de: Sie setzt sich neben mich.
example_sentences.en: She sits down next to me.

case_notes: Two-way: Dativ = location between two things, Akkusativ = movement into a position between.

example_sentences.de: Die Bank steht zwischen den Bäumen.
example_sentences.en: The bench stands between the trees.

example_sentences.de: Er stellt den Stuhl zwischen die Tische.
example_sentences.en: He puts the chair between the tables.

case_notes: Always Akkusativ. Used for opposition, approximate time, and collisions.

example_sentences.de: Er ist gegen die Idee.
example_sentences.en: He is against the idea.

example_sentences.de: Das Spiel beginnt gegen drei Uhr.
example_sentences.en: The game starts around three o'clock.

case_notes: Always Dativ. Used for exclusion and set phrases (außer Betrieb = out of service).

example_sentences.de: Alle kommen außer ihm.
example_sentences.en: Everyone is coming except him.

example_sentences.de: Der Aufzug ist außer Betrieb.
example_sentences.en: The elevator is out of service.

case_notes: Always Dativ. Used for a starting point in time or place.

example_sentences.de: Ab dem ersten Januar gilt die neue Regel.
example_sentences.en: As of January 1st the new rule applies.

example_sentences.de: Der Zug fährt ab dem nächsten Bahnhof nicht mehr.
example_sentences.en: The train no longer runs from the next station.

case_notes: Usually Akkusativ when placed after the noun (den Fluss entlang). Can also govern Dativ when placed before the noun (entlang dem Fluss) — but post-position is far more common.

example_sentences.de: Wir gehen den Fluss entlang.
example_sentences.en: We walk along the river.

example_sentences.de: Fahren Sie die Straße entlang bis zur Ampel.
example_sentences.en: Drive along the road to the traffic light.

case_notes: Formally Genitiv. In colloquial German often used with Dativ (wegen dem Regen instead of wegen des Regens). Formal written German requires Genitiv.

example_sentences.de: Wegen des Regens bleiben wir zu Hause.
example_sentences.en: Because of the rain we stay at home.

example_sentences.de: Der Flug wurde wegen des Sturms gestrichen.
example_sentences.en: The flight was cancelled due to the storm.

case_notes: Can take Dativ or Genitiv. Dativ is standard in modern usage. Expresses positive cause.

example_sentences.de: Dank deiner Hilfe habe ich es geschafft.
example_sentences.en: Thanks to your help I managed it.

example_sentences.de: Dank dem guten Wetter war der Ausflug ein Erfolg.
example_sentences.en: Thanks to the good weather the trip was a success.

case_notes: Always Dativ. Can be placed before or after the noun (gegenüber dem Bahnhof / dem Bahnhof gegenüber). When used with a pronoun it always follows (mir gegenüber).

example_sentences.de: Das Hotel liegt gegenüber dem Bahnhof.
example_sentences.en: The hotel is opposite the train station.

example_sentences.de: Er war mir gegenüber immer freundlich.
example_sentences.en: He was always friendly toward me.

case_notes: Takes Dativ when no article, Genitiv or Dativ with an article. Commonly used in formal or journalistic writing.

example_sentences.de: Laut Bericht hat er keine Fehler gemacht.
example_sentences.en: According to the report he made no mistakes.

example_sentences.de: Laut dem Chef beginnt die Sitzung um zehn.
example_sentences.en: According to the boss the meeting starts at ten.

case_notes: Formally Genitiv. In colloquial speech sometimes used with Dativ (trotz dem Regen). Formal writing requires Genitiv.

example_sentences.de: Trotz des schlechten Wetters gingen wir spazieren.
example_sentences.en: Despite the bad weather we went for a walk.

example_sentences.de: Sie hat trotz ihrer Müdigkeit weitergearbeitet.
example_sentences.en: She kept working despite her tiredness.

case_notes: Genitiv. Very commonly used in written German. Also functions as a conjunction (while/whereas).

example_sentences.de: Während des Unterrichts ist Stille geboten.
example_sentences.en: During the lesson silence is required.

example_sentences.de: Während seines Urlaubs hat er viel gelesen.
example_sentences.en: During his holiday he read a lot.

case_notes: Genitiv. Also written as 'anstatt' with identical meaning and usage. Expresses substitution.

example_sentences.de: Ich nehme Tee statt des Kaffees.
example_sentences.en: I will have tea instead of the coffee.

example_sentences.de: Statt einer Antwort gab er nur ein Lächeln.
example_sentences.en: Instead of an answer he gave only a smile.

case_notes: Genitiv. Used for both spatial (inside a place) and temporal (within a time frame) meaning.

example_sentences.de: Die Pakete werden innerhalb von zwei Tagen geliefert.
example_sentences.en: The parcels will be delivered within two days.

example_sentences.de: Innerhalb des Gebäudes ist Rauchen verboten.
example_sentences.en: Smoking is forbidden inside the building.

case_notes: Genitiv. Used for location or context outside a boundary. Opposite of innerhalb.

example_sentences.de: Er wohnt außerhalb der Stadt.
example_sentences.en: He lives outside the city.

example_sentences.de: Das liegt außerhalb meiner Zuständigkeit.
example_sentences.en: That is outside my area of responsibility.

english: due to, on the basis of, because of
case_notes: Genitiv. Common in written and formal German. Also written as 'auf Grund' (two words).

example_sentences.de: Aufgrund der Verspätung mussten wir warten.
example_sentences.en: Due to the delay we had to wait.

example_sentences.de: Aufgrund seiner Erfahrung bekam er den Job.
example_sentences.en: On the basis of his experience he got the job.

case_notes: Genitiv. Used for location above a reference point on a vertical scale. More precise than 'über'.

example_sentences.de: Das Dorf liegt oberhalb der Baumgrenze.
example_sentences.en: The village lies above the tree line.

example_sentences.de: Die Temperatur bleibt oberhalb des Gefrierpunkts.
example_sentences.en: The temperature stays above the freezing point.

case_notes: Genitiv. Used for location below a reference point on a vertical scale. More precise than 'unter'.

example_sentences.de: Die Höhle liegt unterhalb des Gipfels.
example_sentences.en: The cave is located below the summit.

example_sentences.de: Temperaturen unterhalb des Nullpunkts sind gefährlich.
example_sentences.en: Temperatures below zero are dangerous.

case_notes: Akkusativ. Formal and literary register. Expresses opposition or contradiction. Do not confuse with 'wieder' (again).

example_sentences.de: Er handelte wider besseres Wissen.
example_sentences.en: He acted against his better judgment.

example_sentences.de: Das geht wider meine Überzeugungen.
example_sentences.en: That goes against my convictions.

case_notes: Dativ. Often placed after the noun (meiner Erwartung entgegen). Expresses that something happens in opposition to something else.

example_sentences.de: Entgegen unserer Erwartung kam er doch.
example_sentences.en: Contrary to our expectation he came after all.

example_sentences.de: Er handelte entgegen den Vorschriften.
example_sentences.en: He acted contrary to the regulations.

case_notes: Dativ. Formal register. Often used in legal, official, and formal texts. Can be placed before or after the noun.

example_sentences.de: Gemäß dem Vertrag zahlt er monatlich.
example_sentences.en: In accordance with the contract he pays monthly.

example_sentences.de: Den Regeln gemäß muss er eine Strafe zahlen.
example_sentences.en: According to the rules he must pay a fine.

case_notes: Dativ. Used to include additional items or persons. Similar to 'mitsamt'. Often used in enumerations.

example_sentences.de: Er kam samt seiner Familie.
example_sentences.en: He came along with his family.

example_sentences.de: Das Haus wurde samt Möbeln verkauft.
example_sentences.en: The house was sold together with the furniture.

case_notes: Dativ. Usually placed after the noun as a postposition (dem Bericht zufolge). Formal register, used in journalism and official texts.

example_sentences.de: Dem Bericht zufolge stieg die Temperatur.
example_sentences.en: According to the report the temperature rose.

example_sentences.de: Laut Zeugen zufolge war er nicht dort.
example_sentences.en: According to the witnesses he was not there.

case_notes: Genitiv. Used for spatial reference on the near side of a boundary. Opposite of 'jenseits'.

example_sentences.de: Das Dorf liegt diesseits der Grenze.
example_sentences.en: The village is on this side of the border.

example_sentences.de: Diesseits des Flusses ist das Land fruchtbar.
example_sentences.en: On this side of the river the land is fertile.

english: on the other side of, beyond
case_notes: Genitiv. Used for spatial reference on the far side of a boundary. Also used figuratively ('beyond understanding').

example_sentences.de: Jenseits des Gebirges liegt eine weite Ebene.
example_sentences.en: Beyond the mountains lies a wide plain.

example_sentences.de: Das liegt jenseits meiner Möglichkeiten.
example_sentences.en: That is beyond my capabilities.

english: as a result of, due to
case_notes: Genitiv. Formal register. Indicates a direct consequence or result. Common in written German.

example_sentences.de: Infolge des Unfalls kam es zu Staus.
example_sentences.en: As a result of the accident there were traffic jams.

example_sentences.de: Infolge seiner Nachlässigkeit entstanden Schäden.
example_sentences.en: Due to his negligence damage was caused.

english: with the help of, by means of
case_notes: Genitiv. Also written as 'mit Hilfe' (two words). Indicates the means or assistance used to achieve something.

example_sentences.de: Mithilfe des Wörterbuchs verstand ich den Text.
example_sentences.en: With the help of the dictionary I understood the text.

example_sentences.de: Mithilfe moderner Technik ist das möglich.
example_sentences.en: With the help of modern technology that is possible.

english: for lack of, due to lack of
case_notes: Genitiv. Formal register. Used in legal and official texts. Indicates something cannot happen because of a lack of something.

example_sentences.de: Mangels Beweisen wurde er freigelassen.
example_sentences.en: For lack of evidence he was released.

example_sentences.de: Mangels einer besseren Alternative entschieden wir uns dafür.
example_sentences.en: Due to lack of a better alternative we decided on it.

case_notes: Genitiv. Formal and ceremonial register. Used for events or occasions. Common in speeches, invitations, and formal texts.

example_sentences.de: Anlässlich seines Geburtstags gab es eine Feier.
example_sentences.en: On the occasion of his birthday there was a celebration.

example_sentences.de: Anlässlich des Jubiläums hielt er eine Rede.
example_sentences.en: On the occasion of the anniversary he gave a speech.

case_notes: Genitiv. Formal register. Very common in business correspondence and official communication. Abbreviated as 'bzgl.' in written German.

example_sentences.de: Bezüglich Ihrer Anfrage senden wir Ihnen die Unterlagen.
example_sentences.en: Regarding your inquiry we are sending you the documents.

example_sentences.de: Es gibt noch Fragen bezüglich des Vertrags.
example_sentences.en: There are still questions regarding the contract.

english: with regard to, in terms of
case_notes: Genitiv. Formal register. Used in professional, academic, and official texts. Often interchangeable with 'bezüglich'.

example_sentences.de: Hinsichtlich der Kosten gibt es noch Unklarheiten.
example_sentences.en: With regard to the costs there are still uncertainties.

example_sentences.de: Hinsichtlich seiner Qualifikation ist er gut geeignet.
example_sentences.en: In terms of his qualifications he is well suited.

english: for the purpose of, in order to
case_notes: Genitiv. Very formal and bureaucratic register. Common in official documents and legal texts. Rarely used in spoken German.

example_sentences.de: Zwecks besserer Verständigung schreiben wir auf Deutsch.
example_sentences.en: For the purpose of better communication we write in German.

example_sentences.de: Er reiste zwecks einer Konferenz nach Wien.
example_sentences.en: He travelled to Vienna for the purpose of a conference.

case_notes: Genitiv. Can also take Dativ when used without a following article. Common in formal and commercial texts. Abbreviated as 'einschl.' or 'inkl.'

example_sentences.de: Der Preis beträgt 200 Euro einschließlich der Mehrwertsteuer.
example_sentences.en: The price is 200 euros including VAT.

example_sentences.de: Alle Mitarbeiter einschließlich der Teilzeitkräfte sind eingeladen.
example_sentences.en: All employees including part-time workers are invited.

case_notes: Genitiv. Formal, primarily used in financial and commercial contexts. Abbreviated as 'abzgl.'

example_sentences.de: Der Nettopreis beträgt 100 Euro abzüglich der Rabatte.
example_sentences.en: The net price is 100 euros minus the discounts.

example_sentences.de: Abzüglich der Steuern bleiben 800 Euro übrig.
example_sentences.en: After deduction of taxes 800 euros remain.
